prompt: The Predicate.negate() result is unexpected. What is the bug?
choices: negate() modifies the predicate in place
Predicate.negate() returns a new Predicate that negates the result; it does not modify the original; the negated predicate must be assigned to a variable to be used
negate() only works on Predicate<Boolean>, not Predicate<Integer>
test(3) throws ClassCastException when negate() has been called
explanation: Functional interfaces are immutable — negate() returns a new Predicate and does not change isEven. isEven.test(3) still tests for evenness: 3%2==0 is false. Fix: Predicate<Integer> isOdd = isEven.negate(); then isOdd.test(3) → true.
